edibility: Choice edible; wild form differs from pale cultivated 'enoki'; eaten in soups/stir-fries/hot pots; rare reports of E. coli contamination on commercial enoki
taste: Mild, slightly fruity; firm crunchy texture
search: Late fall to winter, dead/dying elm/willow/poplar/ash; ID clues include orange-brown sticky cap and blackish velvet stem; look-alikes including DEADLY Galerina marginata

toxicity.antidote: N/A — not toxic if correctly identified; verify with spore print
